title: Guard visibility->sim coupling Phase C default-flip blocked on combat-field oracle (M2.3)

history.note: RE-VALIDATION REQUIRED before ACT (FID-0062): this finding was seeded from compare_combat_trace --align move, which has a ~2x sim-tick skew (record-index vs sim-tick). RE-CAPTURE dam route both-sides under --align tick (now the trustworthy combat mode, combat_oracle_fields.md#12) before root-causing. Much of the reported guard divergence is likely alignment artifact + the systemic RNG call-count phase (FID-0063), NOT a chr.c/stan.c logic bug.

Coupling evidence from the FID-0054 investigation (2026-07-11): native PROPFLAG_ONSCREEN is set far more broadly than stock on dam_combat_guard6 (e.g. patrol guards chr 2/3/39/40 onscreen 68.6% of native ticks vs 0% of stock ticks; position divergence confounds a direct flag comparison but the asymmetry direction is stable). The flag feeds act_patrol.lastvisible60 and hence the WAYMODE_MAGIC gate in chrlvTickPatrol (0x7F032548), so this finding co-gates FID-0054 guard-state parity with FID-0014 (see docs/fidelity/derivations/FID-0054-guard-state.md §5.3 and the BACKLOG M2.5 sequencing note: the M2.5 ONSCREEN fix must land with or before the M2.3 Phase-C flip).
